description_long: This object is a classic chessboard set on a flat rectangular base, featuring a checkered playing surface with alternating black and white squares. The board is bordered by a plain, slightly raised edge that frames the playing area without any visible ornamentation or inscriptions. Arranged on the board are two full sets of chess pieces, one in black and the other in white, each occupying their respective starting positions. The chess pieces are traditional in design, with distinguishable forms for pawns, rooks, knights, bishops, queens, and kings, all standing upright on their corresponding squares. The surface of the board and the pieces appear to be made of a hard, glossy material, likely plastic or lacquered wood, giving the set a polished and reflective finish. The overall size and proportions suggest it is suitable for tabletop or park use, making it a familiar sight in urban recreational spaces.
description: A classic chessboard with a checkered black-and-white pattern, bordered by a plain edge and set with two full sets of traditional chess pieces in black and white. The board and pieces have a glossy, polished finish.
description_view_0: Front view showing the white pieces closest and the full arrangement of both black and white chess pieces on the checkered board.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the profile of the chess pieces and the alternating squares, with both sets of pieces visible in a line.
description_view_2: Rear view with the black pieces closest, mirroring the front view but from the opposite side, showing the full arrangement of pieces.
description_view_3: Opposite side view to view 1, showing the profile of the chess pieces from the other side and the checkered pattern.
attribute_board_pattern: The playing surface features a regular grid of alternating black and white squares, arranged in a classic checkered pattern with sharp, clean edges and no decorative elements or markings within the squares.
attribute_border_design: The border is a plain, slightly raised edge surrounding the playing area, matching the color of the lighter squares, with no visible ornamentation, inscriptions, or decorative markings. The width is minimal, just enough to frame the grid.
